title: Work and Time
subtitle: Work Rates, Efficiency, Man-Days, Pipes & Cisterns, Alternate Days

readingSections.title: What is Work and Time?
readingSections.content: Work and Time problems are about finding how long it takes for one or more people to complete a task. The key relationship is: Work Done = Time × Rate of Work. If a person completes a work in X days, then in 1 day they complete 1/X of the work.

subsections.title: The Golden Rule: Work = Time × Rate
subsections.content: Every Work and Time problem reduces to one step: express each worker's output as a fraction of the job per day, then add or subtract the fractions.

subsections.title: The LCM Method (Shortcut)
subsections.content: To avoid fractions, take the LCM of all individual times as the total work. Then find each person's efficiency as: LCM ÷ individual time.

subsections.title: Efficiency = 1/Time
subsections.content: Efficiency and time are inversely proportional. If A is twice as efficient as B, A takes half the time of B.

subsections.title: Man-Days Concept
subsections.content: Total work can be measured in Man-Days (or Man-Hours). If M men work for D days, total work = M × D man-days.

readingSections.title: Combined Work Problems
readingSections.content: When two or more people work together, add their individual rates to find the combined rate. There are three main types of combined work problems.

subsections.title: Type 1: Two Workers Together
subsections.content: Given individual times of two workers, find time when they work together. Together rate = 1/A + 1/B. Time = 1/(1/A + 1/B) = ab/(a+b).

subsections.title: Type 2: One Worker Leaves Early
subsections.content: A and B work together for some days, then one leaves. Find remaining work first, then time for remaining worker.

subsections.title: Type 3: Three Workers Together
subsections.content: Given three individual times, find combined time using LCM method.

readingSections.title: Efficiency & Ratio Problems
readingSections.content: Efficiency is the amount of work done per unit time. More efficiency = less time. Efficiency is inversely proportional to time taken.

subsections.title: Efficiency Ratio to Time Ratio
subsections.content: If efficiency ratio = a : b, then time ratio = b : a.

subsections.title: Difference in Time Given
subsections.content: Sometimes the difference in time taken is given. Use the efficiency ratio to set up equations.

subsections.title: Efficiency Percentage
subsections.content: Sometimes efficiency is given as a percentage of another worker's efficiency.

readingSections.title: Pipes & Cisterns (Special Case)
readingSections.content: Pipes and cistern problems are similar to Work and Time. Inlet pipes fill (positive work), outlet pipes empty (negative work).

subsections.title: Inlet and Outlet Pipes
subsections.content: Inlet pipe: fills the tank → positive rate. Outlet pipe: empties the tank → negative rate. Net rate = Sum(Inlet) - Sum(Outlet).

subsections.title: Alternate Pipes
subsections.content: When pipes are opened alternately, calculate work done in each cycle.

readingSections.title: Alternate Day Work
readingSections.content: When workers take turns working on alternate days, find the work done in each cycle and repeat.

subsections.title: Standard Alternate Day
subsections.content: Two workers work alternately, starting with one of them. Find work done in one 2-day cycle, then repeat.

subsections.title: Three Workers Alternate
subsections.content: Three workers work in a cycle (A, B, C, A, B, C...). Find work done in one 3-day cycle.

formulas.title: Basic Work Rate Formula
formulas.explanation: If a person completes work in X days, their one-day work = 1/X. Work done and time taken are inversely proportional.
formulas.example: A completes work in 10 days → A's rate = 1/10 per day.

formulas.title: Combined Work Formula
formulas.explanation: If A takes 'a' days and B takes 'b' days, together they take ab/(a+b) days.
formulas.example: A takes 10 days, B takes 15 days → Together = (10×15)/(10+15) = 150/25 = 6 days.

formulas.title: Man-Days Formula
formulas.formula: Total Work = M₁ × D₁ = M₂ × D₂
formulas.explanation: If M men take D days, total work = M × D man-days. If workforce changes, M₁D₁ = M₂D₂.
formulas.example: 25 men take 12 days → Total = 300 man-days. 30 men will take 300/30 = 10 days.

formulas.title: Efficiency Ratio Formula
formulas.explanation: If A is twice as efficient as B, A takes half the time of B. Efficiency ratio is the reverse of time ratio.
formulas.example: A is 3 times as efficient as B. B takes 12 days → A takes 12/3 = 4 days.

formulas.title: Pipes & Cisterns Formula
formulas.formula: Net Rate = Sum(Inlet Rates) - Sum(Outlet Rates)
formulas.explanation: If an inlet fills in A hours, rate = 1/A. If an outlet empties in B hours, rate = -1/B.
formulas.example: Pipe A fills in 4 hours, Pipe B empties in 6 hours. Net = 1/4 - 1/6 = 1/12 → 12 hours to fill.

formulas.title: Alternate Days Formula
formulas.formula: Work per cycle = Sum of rates of workers in one cycle
formulas.explanation: If A and B work alternately, one cycle = 2 days (A's day + B's day). Find work done per cycle and repeat.

formulas.title: Men-Women-Children Formula
formulas.formula: Equate work done: M₁ × E₁ = M₂ × E₂ (where E = efficiency)
formulas.explanation: Find the efficiency ratio between different categories first, then convert all workers to a common unit.
formulas.example: 2 men = 3 women in efficiency. Convert all to men (or women) then solve.

basic-work-rate.q: A can do a piece of work in 10 days. What is the amount of work done by A in 1 day?
s: If a person completes work in X days, work done in 1 day = 1/X.

basic-work-rate.q: B can complete a work in 15 days. How much work does B complete in 3 days?
A does 1/4 of a work in 5 days. In how many days will A complete the full work?
A does a work in 8 days. What part of work does A do in 2 days?
A can do a work in 30 days. What fraction of work is left after A works for 10 days?
A alone does 60% of a work in 12 days. How many days will A take to complete the full work?
A can complete a work in 24 days. What fraction of work does A complete in 4 days?
A can do a work in 16 days. What fraction of work is left after A works for 12 days?
A completes 2/5 of a work in 8 days. In how many days will A complete the full work?
A can do a work in 28 days. What fraction of work does A do in 7 days?

combined-work.q: A can do a work in 12 days. B can do the same work in 18 days. What is A and B's combined one-day work?
A takes 10 days and B takes 15 days to complete a work. In how many days will they complete it together?
A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. A alone can complete it in 20 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A and B together can complete a work in 8 days. If A alone takes 12 days, what is B's rate?
A and B together can complete a work in 15 days. They work together for 10 days. What fraction of work is left?
A and B together can complete a work in 16 days. A alone takes 24 days. In how many days can B alone complete the work?
A can do a work in 8 days. B can do it in 12 days. In how many days can they complete it together?
A and B together can complete a work in 20 days. They work together for 8 days. What fraction of work is completed?
A and B together can complete a work in 24 days. B alone takes 36 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A and B can complete a work in 18 days and 24 days respectively. They work together for 6 days. What fraction of work is left?

man-days.q: If 10 men can complete a work in 20 days, how many men are needed to complete it in 10 days?
20 men can complete a work in 15 days. How many man-days are required for the work?
If 5 men can complete a work in 12 days, how many days will 6 men take?
8 men can complete a work in 15 days. How many men are required to complete it in 12 days?
10 men can complete a work in 20 days. After 10 days, 5 more men join. How many more days will it take to complete the work?
25 men can complete a work in 20 days. How many men are needed to complete it in 10 days?
15 men can complete a work in 24 days. How many days will 20 men take?
30 men can complete a work in 12 days. After 4 days, 10 more men join. How many more days will it take?
12 men can complete a work in 20 days. How many days will 15 men take?
20 men can complete a work in 30 days. After 10 days, 5 men leave. How many more days will the remaining men take?

efficiency-ratio.q: A is twice as efficient as B. B takes 24 days to complete a work. How many days does A take?
s: A is twice efficient → A takes half the time of B.

efficiency-ratio.q: A is 3 times as efficient as B. If B takes 12 days, how many days does A take?
s: A is 3 times efficient → A takes 1/3 of B's time.

efficiency-ratio.q: A is 4 times as efficient as B and finishes work 90 days less than B. How many days does A take alone?
s: Efficiency ratio A:B = 4:1 → Time ratio A:B = 1:4.
Let A take x days, B take 4x days.

efficiency-ratio.q: A is 50% more efficient than B. If B takes 24 days, how many days does A take?
A is 25% more efficient than B. B can complete a work in 25 days. In how many days can A complete the work?
A is 40% more efficient than B. If B takes 28 days, how many days will A take?
A is 20% less efficient than B. If B takes 20 days, how many days does A take?
A is 60% more efficient than B. If B takes 40 days, how many days will A take?
A is thrice as efficient as B. Together they complete a work in 6 days. In how many days can A complete the work alone?
A is twice as efficient as B. Together they complete a work in 12 days. In how many days can A alone complete it?

pipes-cisterns.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. How much of the tank does it fill in 1 hour?
s: If a pipe fills in X hours, in 1 hour it fills 1/X.

pipes-cisterns.q: A pipe fills a tank in 4 hours, another empties it in 6 hours. If both are opened, what is the net rate?
A pipe fills a tank in 5 hours, another empties it in 7 hours. If both are opened, how long to fill?
A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. Due to a leak, it takes 12 hours to fill. What is the leak's rate?
A pipe can fill a tank in 10 hours. A leak can empty it in 15 hours. If both are opened, how long to fill?
A pipe fills a tank in 3 hours, another empties it in 5 hours. If the tank is 2/3 full and both are opened, how long to empty?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 8 hours. The tank is 1/2 full. If both are opened, how long to empty?
Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 4 hours and 6 hours respectively. How much do they fill together in 1 hour?
A pipe fills a tank in 5 hours. Another pipe empties it in 10 hours. If both are opened, how long to fill?
A pipe fills a tank in 7 hours. A leak empties it in 9 hours. The tank is 3/4 full. If both are opened, how long to empty?

alternate-days.q: A takes 6 days and B takes 12 days. They work alternately starting with A. What is the work done in the first 2 days?
A takes 10 days and B takes 15 days. They work alternately starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
A takes 12 days and B takes 18 days. They work alternately starting with A. What is the work done in 6 days?
A takes 8 days and B takes 12 days. They work alternately starting with B. What is the work done in first 2 days?
A takes 15 days and B takes 20 days. They work alternately starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?

men-women-children.q: A man, a woman, and a child can complete a work in 10, 15, and 20 days respectively. In how many days can 2 men and 1 woman complete the work together?
5 men can complete a work in 16 days. 8 women can complete the same work in 20 days. In how many days can 10 men and 4 women complete the work?
A man is twice as efficient as a woman. A woman is thrice as efficient as a child. If 2 men can complete a work in 6 days, how many days will 4 women take?
2 men and 3 women can complete a work in 10 days. 3 men and 2 women can complete it in 8 days. In how many days can 1 man and 1 woman complete it?
s: Let man's rate = m, woman's rate = w.
Solve: multiply first by 3, second by 2.

men-women-children.q: A man and a woman together can complete a work in 12 days. The man alone can complete it in 18 days. In how many days can the woman alone complete it?

mcqs.q: A can do a piece of work in 10 days. What is the amount of work done by A in 1 day?
mcqs.exp: If a person completes a work in X days, work done in 1 day = 1/X. Here X = 10, so 1 day work = 1/10.

mcqs.q: B can complete a work in 15 days. How much work does B complete in 3 days?
A does 1/4 of a work in 5 days. In how many days will A complete the full work?
mcqs.exp: If 1/4 work takes 5 days, full work (1) takes 5 × 4 = 20 days.

mcqs.q: A can do a work in 12 days. B can do the same work in 18 days. What is A and B's combined one-day work?
A takes 10 days and B takes 15 days to complete a work. In how many days will they complete it together?
A can complete a work in 20 days. B is twice as efficient as A. In how many days can B complete the work alone?
mcqs.exp: B is twice as efficient → B takes half the time of A. B's time = 20/2 = 10 days.

mcqs.q: If 10 men can complete a work in 20 days, how many men are needed to complete it in 10 days?
mcqs.exp: Total work = 10 × 20 = 200 man-days. For 10 days: men = 200/10 = 20 men.

mcqs.q: A does a work in 8 days. What part of work does A do in 2 days?
A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. A alone can complete it in 20 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A is 3 times as efficient as B. If B takes 24 days to complete a work, how many days does A take?
mcqs.exp: A is 3 times efficient → A takes 1/3 of B's time. A = 24/3 = 8 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. How much of the tank does it fill in 1 hour?
mcqs.exp: If a pipe fills in X hours, in 1 hour it fills 1/X of the tank. Here = 1/6.

mcqs.q: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 4 hours and 6 hours respectively. How much of the tank do they fill together in 1 hour?
A can do a work in 30 days. What fraction of work is left after A works for 10 days?
20 men can complete a work in 15 days. How many man-days are required for the work?
A and B together can complete a work in 8 days. If A alone takes 12 days, what is B's rate?
If 5 men can complete a work in 12 days, how many days will 6 men take?
mcqs.exp: Total work = 5 × 12 = 60 man-days. 6 men: days = 60/6 = 10 days.

mcqs.q: A can do a work in 18 days. B can do the same work in 12 days. Who is more efficient?
mcqs.exp: Less time = more efficiency. B takes 12 days < A takes 18 days, so B is more efficient.

mcqs.q: A pipe fills a tank in 8 hours. Another pipe empties the tank in 12 hours. If both are opened together, what is the net rate?
mcqs.exp: Fill rate = 1/8, Empty rate = 1/12. Net = 1/8 - 1/12 = (3-2)/24 = 1/24 fill.

mcqs.q: A alone does 60% of a work in 12 days. How many days will A take to complete the full work?
A and B together can complete a work in 15 days. They work together for 10 days. What fraction of work is left?
If the efficiency of A is twice that of B, and B takes 20 days to complete a work, how many days will A and B together take?
mcqs.exp: B takes 20 days → B's rate = 1/20. A is twice efficient → A's rate = 2/20 = 1/10. Together = 1/10 + 1/20 = 3/20. Time = 20/3 = 6.67 days.

mcqs.q: A can complete a work in 24 days. B is 50% more efficient than A. How many days will B take alone?
mcqs.exp: B is 50% more efficient → B's efficiency = 1.5 × A's efficiency. B's time = 24/1.5 = 16 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 10 hours. Due to a leak, it takes 15 hours to fill. What is the leak's rate?
mcqs.exp: Pipe rate = 1/10. Net rate with leak = 1/15. Leak rate = 1/10 - 1/15 = (3-2)/30 = 1/30.

mcqs.q: A takes 6 days and B takes 12 days to complete a work. They work alternately starting with A. What is the work done in the first 2 days?
10 men can complete a work in 20 days. After 10 days, 5 more men join. How many more days will it take to complete the work?
mcqs.exp: Total work = 10 × 20 = 200 man-days. In 10 days, work done = 10 × 10 = 100. Remaining = 100. Now 15 men: days = 100/15 = 6.67 days.

mcqs.q: A can do a work in 8 days. B can do it in 12 days. In how many days can they complete it together?
If A is 4 times as efficient as B, and A takes 15 days less than B, how many days does A take alone?
mcqs.exp: Efficiency ratio A:B = 4:1 → Time ratio = 1:4. Let A take x days, B take 4x days. 4x - x = 15 → 3x = 15 → x = 5 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe fills a tank in 5 hours. Another pipe empties the tank in 7 hours. If both are opened, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A can complete a work in 16 days. What fraction of work does A complete in 4 days?
8 men can complete a work in 15 days. How many men are required to complete it in 12 days?
mcqs.exp: Total work = 8 × 15 = 120 man-days. For 12 days: men = 120/12 = 10 men.

mcqs.q: A and B can complete a work in 12 days and 18 days respectively. A started the work and worked for 6 days. Then B joined. In how many days was the work completed?
mcqs.exp: A's 6 days work = 6/12 = 1/2. Remaining = 1/2. A+B rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36. Time for remaining = (1/2)/(5/36) = 18/5 = 3.6 days. Total = 6+3.6 = 9.6 days.

mcqs.q: A is 25% more efficient than B. B can complete a work in 25 days. In how many days can A complete the work?
mcqs.exp: A is 25% more efficient = 1.25 × B's efficiency. A's time = 25/1.25 = 20 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 12 minutes. A leak can empty it in 20 minutes. If both are opened, how long will it take to fill the tank?
mcqs.exp: Fill rate = 1/12, Empty rate = 1/20. Net = 1/12 - 1/20 = (5-3)/60 = 2/60 = 1/30. Time = 30 minutes.

mcqs.q: A and B together can complete a work in 20 days. A alone can complete it in 30 days. If B works alone for 10 days, what fraction of work will be completed?
A and B together can do a work in 8 days. B and C together can do it in 12 days. A and C together can do it in 16 days. In how many days can A, B, and C together complete the work?
A is twice as efficient as B. Together they complete a work in 12 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: A = 2B. A+B = 2B+B = 3B = 1/12. B = 1/36. A = 2/36 = 1/18. A alone takes 18 days.

mcqs.q: A and B together can complete a work in 15 days. A alone takes 20 days. How many days will B take to complete the work?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 8 hours. If the tank is half full and both pipes are opened, how long will it take to fill the tank completely?
mcqs.exp: Fill rate = 1/6, Empty rate = 1/8. Net = 1/6 - 1/8 = (4-3)/24 = 1/24. Half tank remains → time = (1/2)/(1/24) = 12 hours.

mcqs.q: A can complete a work in 10 days, B in 15 days, and C in 20 days. They work together for 3 days, then C leaves. How many more days will A and B take to complete the remaining work?
A is 40% more efficient than B. If B takes 28 days to complete a work, how many days will A take?
mcqs.exp: A is 40% more efficient = 1.4 × B. A's time = 28/1.4 = 20 days.

mcqs.q: A and B can complete a work in 24 days and 36 days respectively. They work together for 12 days. What fraction of the work is completed?
Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 10 hours and 15 hours respectively. A third pipe C can empty it in 12 hours. If all three are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A takes 12 days, B takes 18 days, C takes 24 days. They work alternately A,B,C,A,B,C... Starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
mcqs.exp: LCM=72. A=6,B=4,C=3. 3-day cycle=13. 5 cycles(15 days)=65. Remaining=7. Day16: A does 6→remaining1. Day17: B→1/4 day. Total=16.25.

mcqs.q: A can do a work in 8 days, B in 12 days. They work together for 3 days. What fraction of work is left?
A is 60% more efficient than B. If A takes 15 days to complete a work, how many days will B take?
mcqs.exp: A is 60% more efficient = 1.6 × B. Time ratio A:B = 1:1.6 = 5:8. B's time = 15 × 8/5 = 24 days.

mcqs.q: A can complete a work in 30 days. B is 50% more efficient than A. How many days will B take to complete the work?
mcqs.exp: B is 50% more efficient → B = 1.5 × A. B's time = 30/1.5 = 20 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. After opening it for 3 hours, another pipe B also opens and the tank fills in 2 more hours. How long would B alone take to fill the tank?
mcqs.exp: A's 5 hours work = 5/8. Remaining = 3/8 filled by B in 2 hours. B's rate = (3/8)/2 = 3/16. B alone takes 16/3 = 5.33 hours.

mcqs.q: A and B together can complete a work in 20 days. B and C together can complete it in 30 days. A and C together can complete it in 40 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A man, a woman, and a child can complete a work in 10 days, 15 days, and 20 days respectively. In how many days can 2 men and 1 woman complete the work together?
A takes 16 days, B takes 24 days to complete a work. They work alternately starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
mcqs.exp: LCM(16,24)=48. A=3,B=2. 2-day cycle=5. 9 cycles(18 days)=45. Remaining=3. Next day A does 3→completes. Total=19 days.

mcqs.q: A can do a work in 18 days. B can do it in 24 days. They work together for 6 days. Then A leaves. How many more days will B take to complete the remaining work?
A is twice as efficient as B. B is thrice as efficient as C. If C takes 30 days to complete a work, how many days will A take?
mcqs.exp: Efficiency: A = 2B, B = 3C → A = 6C. Time ratio A:C = 1:6. A's time = 30/6 = 5 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. A leak can empty it in 10 hours. If the tank is 2/3 full and both are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank?
A and B together can complete a work in 16 days. A alone takes 24 days. In how many days can B alone complete the work?
A is 25% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 8 days. In how many days can A complete the work alone?
A can complete a work in 12 days. B can complete it in 18 days. C can complete it in 24 days. They work together for 4 days. What fraction of work is left?
5 men can complete a work in 16 days. 8 women can complete the same work in 20 days. In how many days can 10 men and 4 women complete the work?
mcqs.exp: 5 men×16=80 man-days. 8 women×20=160 woman-days. 1 man=2 women. 10 men+4 women=12 men. Days=80/12=6.67.

mcqs.q: A and B together can complete a work in 24 days. They work together for 8 days, then A leaves. B completes the remaining work in 24 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: A+B=1/24. In 8 days=1/3. Remaining=2/3. B does 2/3 in 24 days→B alone=36 days. A=1/24-1/36=1/72. A takes 72 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe fills a tank in 5 hours. Another pipe fills the same tank in 7 hours. A third pipe empties it in 10 hours. If all three are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A can do a work in 14 days. B is twice as efficient as A. They work together for 2 days. What fraction of work is completed?
A is 33.33% more efficient than B. If B takes 24 days to complete a work, how many days will A take?
A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. They work together for 6 days. Then A leaves and B completes the work in 18 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: In 6 days=6/12=1/2. Remaining=1/2. B does 1/2 in 18 days→B alone=36 days. A=1/12-1/36=1/18. A takes 18 days.

mcqs.q: A and B can complete a work in 18 days and 24 days respectively. They work alternately starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
mcqs.exp: LCM=72. A=4,B=3. 2-day cycle=7. 10 cycles(20)=70. Remaining=2. Day21: A does 4→2/4=0.5. Total=20.5 days.

mcqs.q: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 8 hours and 12 hours respectively. Both are opened for 4 hours, then B is closed. How many more hours will A take to fill the tank?
A is 20% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 10 days. In how many days can B alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 20 days. B can complete it in 30 days. They work together for 5 days. Then A leaves. How many more days will B take to complete the remaining work?
A pipe fills a tank in 4 hours. Another pipe empties it in 6 hours. If the tank is empty and both are opened, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A and B together can complete a work in 30 days. A alone takes 45 days. In how many days can B alone complete the work?
A is thrice as efficient as B. Together they complete a work in 6 days. In how many days can A complete the work alone?
A and B can complete a work in 15 days and 20 days respectively. They work together for 4 days. What fraction of work is left?

A, B, and C can complete a work in 10, 15, and 20 days respectively. They work together for 2 days, then A leaves. B and C continue for 3 more days, then B leaves. How many more days will C take to complete the remaining work?
A is 50% more efficient than B. C is 20% less efficient than B. If A, B, C together can complete a work in 12 days, in how many days can B alone complete the work?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. Due to a leak, it takes 9 hours to fill. In how many hours can the leak empty the full tank?
mcqs.exp: Pipe=1/6, Net=1/9. Leak=1/6-1/9=1/18. Leak empties in 18 hours.

mcqs.q: A and B together can complete a work in 20 days. B and C together can complete it in 30 days. A and C together can complete it in 40 days. In how many days can A, B, and C together complete the work?
A, B, and C can complete a work in 8, 12, and 16 days respectively. They work alternately A,B,C,A,B,C... Starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
mcqs.exp: LCM=48. A=6,B=4,C=3. 3-day=13. 3 cycles(9)=39. Remaining=9. Day10:A→6, rem3. Day11:B→3/4=0.75. Total=10.75.

mcqs.q: A is 60% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 8 days. In how many days can B alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 10 days. B is 20% less efficient than A. How many days will B take to complete the work?
mcqs.exp: B=0.8×A. Time ratio A:B=0.8:1=4:5. B's time=10×5/4=12.5 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 12 hours. The tank is 1/4 full. If both pipes are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank completely?
A and B together can complete a work in 24 days. They work together for 6 days. Then A leaves. B completes the remaining work in 30 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: In 6 days=1/4. Remaining=3/4. B does 3/4 in 30 days→B alone=40 days. A=1/24-1/40=1/60. A takes 60 days.

mcqs.q: A can do a work in 15 days. B is 25% more efficient than A. How many days will B take to complete the same work?

A, B, and C can complete a work in 10, 15, and 30 days respectively. They work together. After 3 days, A leaves. B and C work together for 2 more days, then B leaves. How many more days will C take to complete the remaining work?
A is twice as efficient as B. B is thrice as efficient as C. If A, B, and C together complete a work in 6 days, in how many days can C alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: A=2B, B=3C→A=6C. A+B+C=10C=1/6→C=1/60. C takes 60 days.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 5 hours. Another pipe can fill it in 7 hours. A third pipe can empty it in 8 hours. If all three are opened together for 2 hours, then the third pipe is closed. How many more hours will it take to fill the tank?
A and B together can complete a work in 18 days. B and C together can complete it in 24 days. A and C together can complete it in 36 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A is 40% less efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 12 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 12 days. B can complete it in 18 days. They work alternately starting with A. In how many days will 3/4 of the work be completed?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 10 hours. The tank is 3/4 full. If both pipes are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank completely?

A, B, and C can complete a work in 12, 16, and 20 days respectively. They work together for 3 days. Then A leaves. B and C work together for 2 more days, then B leaves. In how many more days will C complete the remaining work?
A is 25% less efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 16 days. In how many days can B alone complete the work?
A and B together can complete a work in 15 days. They work together for 5 days. Then A leaves. B completes the remaining work in 20 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: In 5 days=1/3. Rem=2/3. B does 2/3 in 20 days→B=30 days. A=1/15-1/30=1/30. A takes 30 days.

mcqs.q: A can complete a work in 20 days. B is 33.33% more efficient than A. How many days will B take to complete the work?
A, B, and C can complete a work in 6, 8, and 12 days respectively. They work alternately A,B,C,A,B,C... Starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
mcqs.exp: LCM=24. A=4,B=3,C=2. 3-day=9. 2 cycles(6)=18. Rem=6. Day7:A→4, rem2. Day8:B→2/3. Total=7.67.

mcqs.q: A pipe can fill a tank in 7 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 9 hours. If the tank is 2/5 full and both pipes are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank completely?
A and B together can complete a work in 10 days. B and C together can complete it in 15 days. A and C together can complete it in 20 days. In how many days can A, B, and C together complete the work?
A is 30% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 10 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 8 days. B can complete it in 12 days. They work together for 2 days. Then A leaves. How many more days will B take to complete the remaining work?
A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. Another pipe can fill it in 12 hours. A third pipe can empty it in 16 hours. If all three are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?

A, B, and C can complete a work in 9, 12, and 18 days respectively. They work together for 2 days. Then A leaves. B and C work together for 3 more days, then B leaves. In how many more days will C complete the remaining work?
A is 50% less efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 18 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 16 days. B is 20% more efficient than A. How many days will B take to complete the work?
A and B can together finish a work in 30 days. They worked together for 20 days and then B left. After another 20 days, A finished the remaining work. In how many days can A alone finish the work?
mcqs.exp: Work done in 20 days=20/30=2/3. Remaining=1/3. A does 1/3 in 20 days→A alone=60 days.

mcqs.q: 20 men can do a piece of work in 50 days. Find the amount of work done by 70 men in 5 days.
mcqs.exp: Total work=20×50=1000 man-days. Work done=70×5=350. Percentage=350/1000×100=35%.

mcqs.q: 54 men can do a work in 12 days. Find the amount of work done by 5 men in 12 days.
43 men can do a work in 89 days. Find the amount of work done by 90 men in 55 days.
64 men can do a piece of work in 90 days. Find the amount of work done by 95 men in 50 days.
65 men can do a piece of work in 87 days. Find the amount of work done by 9 men in 125 days.
A can do a piece of work in 10 days, B in 15 days. They work together for 3 days. How much work is left?
A and B together can do a work in 12 days. A alone can do it in 20 days. In how many days can B alone do it?
A does 60% of work in 12 days. How many more days to complete full work?
A can do a work in 8 days, B can do the same in 12 days. They work together for 4 days. What fraction of work is left?
A can do a work in 15 days and B can do it in 20 days. With help of C, they finish it in 5 days. How long will C alone take?
A group of 123 men can do a piece of work in 21 days. Find the amount of work done by 32 men in 99 days.
A group of 9 men can do a piece of work in 45 days. Find the amount of work done by 5 men in 20 days.
A and B can complete a work in 12 days and 18 days respectively. A started the work and worked for 4 days. Then B joined. In how many days was the work completed?
A and B can complete a work in 24 days and 36 days respectively. They work together for 8 days. What fraction of work is completed?
A and B together can complete a work in 8 days. A alone can complete it in 12 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A is twice as efficient as B. Together they complete a work in 12 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A and B together can complete a work in 30 days. They work together for 20 days. Then B leaves. A completes the remaining work in 20 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: In 20 days=20/30=2/3. Rem=1/3. A does 1/3 in 20 days→A alone=60 days.

mcqs.q: A can complete a work in 10 days. B can complete it in 15 days. C can complete it in 20 days. They work together. After 2 days, A leaves. How many more days will B and C take to complete the remaining work?
A is 25% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 10 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 12 hours. The tank is 1/2 full. If both pipes are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank completely?
A can do a work in 14 days. B can do it in 21 days. They work together. After 7 days, A leaves. How many more days will B take to complete the remaining work?
A and B together can complete a work in 16 days. B and C together can complete it in 24 days. A and C together can complete it in 32 days. In how many days can A, B, and C together complete the work?
A is 40% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 12 days. In how many days can A alone complete it?
A pipe can fill a tank in 5 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 7 hours. The tank is 3/4 empty. If both pipes are opened, how long will it take to fill the tank completely?
A and B together can complete a work in 18 days. They work together for 6 days. Then A leaves. B completes the remaining work in 24 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
mcqs.exp: In 6d=1/3. Rem=2/3. B does 2/3 in 24d→B=36d. A=1/18-1/36=1/36. A takes 36 days.

mcqs.q: A can complete a work in 12 days. B can complete it in 18 days. They work alternately starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 8 hours. The tank is 1/3 full. If both pipes are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank completely?

A, B, and C can complete a work in 8, 12, and 16 days respectively. They work together for 3 days. Then A leaves. B and C work together for 2 more days, then B leaves. How many more days will C take to complete the remaining work?
A is 20% less efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 15 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A and B together can complete a work in 20 days. They work together for 8 days. Then A leaves. B completes the remaining work in 18 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 18 days. B is 50% more efficient than A. How many days will B take to complete the work?
A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. B and C together can complete it in 18 days. A and C together can complete it in 24 days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A is 30% less efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 14 days. In how many days can A alone complete it?
A pipe can fill a tank in 4 hours. Another pipe can empty it in 6 hours. The tank is 5/6 full. If both pipes are opened, how long will it take to empty the tank completely?

A, B, and C can complete a work in 10, 15, and 25 days respectively. They work together. After 5 days, A leaves. B and C work together for 3 more days, then B leaves. How many more days will C take to complete the remaining work?
A is 60% more efficient than B. Together they complete a work in 8 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A and B together can complete a work in 15 days. They work together for 10 days. Then A leaves. B completes the remaining work in 15 more days. In how many days can A alone complete the work?
A can complete a work in 20 days. B is 25% more efficient than A. How many days will B take?
A and B together can complete a work in 24 days. A alone takes 36 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
20 men can do a work in 50 days. How many days will 25 men take?
A can do a work in 25 days. B can do it in 20 days. They work together for 5 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take?
A pipe fills a tank in 3 hours, B fills in 4 hours, C empties in 6 hours. All opened together, how long to fill?
A can do a work in 10 days, B in 12 days, C in 15 days. A and B work together for 4 days, then C replaces A. How many more days?
A is 50% more efficient than B. B takes 30 days. A and B work together for 4 days, then B leaves. How many more days will A take?
A and B together can complete a work in 40 days. A alone takes 60 days. In how many days can B alone complete it?
A can do a work in 28 days. B can do it in 35 days. They work together for 7 days, then A leaves. How many more days will B take?
Two pipes A and B fill a tank in 5 and 6 hours. A third pipe C empties in 8 hours. All opened, how long to fill?
A can do a work in 12 days, B in 18 days. They work alternately starting with B. In how many days will the work be completed?
mcqs.exp: LCM=36. A=3,B=2. 2-day=5. 7 cycles(14)=35. Rem=1. B does 2 units next→0.5 day. Total=14.5.

mcqs.q: A, B, and C can complete a work in 6, 9, and 15 days respectively. They work together for 2 days. Then A leaves. B and C work together for 2 more days, then B leaves. How many more days will C take?
